input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page in its own section -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: two tabs (Active / Archived) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml setup with:
1. A flyout menu with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. A Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage

I'll create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files showing this structure.

    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"

    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page in its own section -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: two tabs (Active / Archived) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the structure:

**AppShell.xaml** – Three `FlyoutItem`s define the flyout menu. The "Products" item wraps a `<Tab>` containing two `ShellContent` entries, which renders as bottom tabs (Active / Archived) when that flyout item is selected.

**AppShell.xaml.cs** – Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a global route so you can navigate to it from any product list:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productdetails?id={product.Id}");
```

To receive the parameter, add `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` to your `ProductDetailsPage` class (or use `IQueryAttributable`).

**Key points:**
- `FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"` enables the hamburger menu.
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until navigated to.
- Routes in the visual tree are automatic; `Routing.RegisterRoute` is only needed for detail pages outside the hierarchy.
